title: andropogon nardus subsp. cerifer
intro: Andropogon nardus subsp. cerifer, also known as Coarse Lemongrass, is a subspecies of the Andropogon nardus plant, a type of grass native to Australia and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Coarse Lemongrass include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of Andropogon nardus subsp. cerifer include infusions, essential oils, and decoctions, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams.
